scope: Invite-only access decisions, password activation/reset, MFA recovery-code boundaries, one-device security history, and secure out-of-band lost-device session revocation

publicCeremony.requestResponse: Eligible, unknown and ineligible syntactically valid email addresses receive the same 202 body. An internal issuance/outbox/audit failure reached after eligibility is also logged with a constant code and returns the exact same status, body and headers, preventing an operational enumeration oracle. Invalid syntax is rejected without an account lookup result.
publicCeremony.eligibility: Only an active learner with a verified email and one unexpired active database session receives a proof email.

proof.rawDerivation: HMAC-SHA-256 over a random request UUID under a dedicated production-required LOST_DEVICE_PROOF_KEY. Development/tests may fall back to the Better Auth secret, but production rejects that fallback; Compose mounts the independent key only into the app and mail worker.
proof.storedVerifier: SHA-256 of the raw proof only; lowercase 64-hex database constraint
proof.singleUse: One conditional PostgreSQL UPDATE sets consumed_at only while it is null and expires_at is strictly later than the verification time.
proof.ownerBinding: The proof row carries user_id and session_id. Verification accepts no caller-supplied target and rechecks the active session belongs to that learner.
proof.delivery: email_outbox stores only learner name and recoveryRequestId. The worker derives the bearer in memory, verifies its stored hash, suppresses expired/tampered delivery, and never writes expanded variables back.
proof.browserTransport: The bearer is placed after # in the email URL so it is absent from HTTP requests and Referer headers. The client captures it before React Strict Mode effect replay and immediately clears browser history.

administratorDecision.queueAdmission: A session_revocation_request is created only after atomic proof consumption and records request_channel=email_proof plus identity_verified_at and proof_request_id.
administratorDecision.identityBoundary: Mailbox control is explicitly insufficient. UI and operator guidance require a separate identity confirmation; approval/rejection retains the existing fresh administrator MFA and recorded-reason gate.
administratorDecision.effects: Approval revokes only the proof-bound active session, archives token-free history as lost_device_approved, audits the decision, and sends durable learner/admin notifications. Rejection also audits and notifies without revocation.

implementation.lifecycle: Account deletion explicitly removes proof-backed revocation requests before RESTRICT-bound lost_device_proof rows, then removes token-free history. Separate PostgreSQL tests cover pending and approved/revoked states and prove no orphan.
implementation.passwordRecovery: Better Auth reset remains a one-time link, returns the same request result for known and unknown addresses, rejects replay, revokes the database session, and now archives it with password_reset rather than a generic logout reason. No password is present in outbox or history evidence.

securityInvariants: No password is generated or emailed during invitation, activation, lost-device, or reset journeys.
No lost-device bearer proof is stored plaintext in lost_device_proof, email_outbox, audit_event, session_revocation_request, notification, or auth_session_history.
No raw proof appears in an HTTP URL, response, audit metadata, or worker log.
An eligible-account issuance failure cannot change the neutral public response or log the email; malformed input and rate-policy responses remain intentionally distinct.
A proof cannot select another learner or session, and corrupted cross-owner binding is consumed without creating a request.
Concurrent issue calls reuse one proof/outbox row; concurrent verify calls produce exactly one consumer and one review request.
Expiry is strict at the exact 15-minute boundary and replay never creates another request.
A stale administrator MFA assertion cannot decide or revoke; a fresh one plus a reason is required.
One-device uniqueness and production fail-closed authentication were not weakened.

postgres.result: 1 file passed; 9 tests passed; 0 failed; migrations 0026 through 0028 applied successfully twice
coverage: 12-way issuance deduplication and no plaintext proof/proof URL in persisted rows
12-way atomic consume race, replay rejection, owner/session binding and admin notification
exact expiry and corrupted cross-owner binding rejection
stale then fresh administrator MFA, reasoned approval, owner-only revocation, notification, audit and token-free history
approved proof/request/history account deletion ordering and orphan check
neutral pending access request, approval, invitation activation, direct-signup bypass and activation replay
known/unknown password-recovery parity, one-time reset, replay rejection, session revocation and password_reset history

browser.result: 2 passed in 3.2 seconds; 0 skipped; 0 unexpected; 0 flaky

verification.releaseAudit: verified: 115 requirements and 61 local links resolved
verification.schemaRegeneration: 89 tables and no schema drift after the final coordinated migrations through 0030

auditImpact.reasonNoStatusPromotion: Live Google/OAuth/linking, access-request mailbox verification before review, lost-authenticator administrator recovery, deployed real-cookie breadth, external mail delivery, and production retention/log/backup evidence remain open.

remainingGaps: The access-request endpoint still does not complete mailbox verification before exposing a pending request to the administrator.
No live Google OAuth call, approved Google-link activation, verified-email linking, callback, or OAuth-to-TOTP bypass journey was executed.
Lost-device recovery revokes the inaccessible session but deliberately does not reset TOTP or reveal/regenerate recovery codes; a separately approved lost-authenticator ceremony remains open.
The full sensitive-command matrix has not been exercised with deployed real learner/admin cookies.
No Gmail/OAuth delivery, bounce/suppression, SPF/DKIM/DMARC, or external mailbox receipt was tested.
No production 90-day retention run or live dump/log/export/backup secret-canary inspection was performed.
